title: un, une ou des ?
lesson: The indefinite article changes according to gender and number: 'un' before a masculine singular noun (un livre), 'une' before a feminine singular noun (une table), and 'des' before a plural noun, masculine or feminine (des chats, des maisons).

items.explanation: 'Livre' is masculine singular, so you use 'un' (un livre).

items.text: Il y a {1} table dans la cuisine.
items.explanation: 'Table' is feminine singular, so you use 'une' (une table).

items.text: Je vois {1} chats dans le jardin.
items.explanation: 'Chats' is plural, so you use 'des' (des chats).
'Pomme' is feminine singular, so you use 'une' (une pomme).
'Chien' is masculine singular, so you use 'un' (un chien).
'Amis' is plural, so you use 'des' (des amis).
'Banane' is feminine singular, so you use 'une' (une banane).

items.text: Il y a {1} stylo sur la table.
items.explanation: 'Stylo' is masculine singular, so you use 'un' (un stylo).
'Voiture' is feminine singular, so you use 'une' (une voiture).

items.text: Elle a {1} fleurs sur la fenêtre.
items.explanation: 'Fleurs' is plural, so you use 'des' (des fleurs).
'Maison' is feminine singular, so you use 'une' (une maison).
'Sac' is masculine singular (un sac); 'chaussures' is plural (des chaussures).
